idea.hook: What if a space station crashed?
facts: It would take 175 school buses lined up to equal the length of the International Space Station's orbit
The ISS orbits Earth at 17,500 mph, or about 25 times faster than a commercial airliner
If it were to crash, debris would spread over an area roughly the size of Texas
idea.payoff: and we still don't know how we'd clean it up
idea.title: Space Crash! 🚀
idea.description: Imagine a space station the size of a football field crashing to Earth 🌎! You're living in a time where space debris is a growing concern. Will humanity be able to prevent a catastrophic space crash, or will we be faced with a massive cleanup effort? 🤔

scenes.text: WHAT IF A SPACE STATION CRASHED?
scenes.visual: A futuristic space station, initially whole, then fragments appearing as if breaking apart, against a dark space background.
An animation showing the ISS orbiting Earth, then a visual representation of 175 school buses lining up to form the circumference of its orbit path.

scenes.text: It orbits at 17,500 mph (25x an airliner).
scenes.visual: The ISS zipping across the screen with a speed trail, then a smaller, slower commercial airliner icon appearing next to it for comparison.

scenes.text: Debris would cover an area the size of TEXAS.
scenes.visual: An overhead view of Earth with the state of Texas highlighted. Space debris particles are animated to spread over and beyond this highlighted area.

scenes.text: And we don't know how to clean it up.
scenes.visual: A dense, overwhelming field of space debris orbiting Earth, looking chaotic and impossible to manage. The debris slowly spins, emphasizing the vastness of the problem.
